message:
Handle multiline paste safely in the TUI (#13612)

## Description

**Stack:** 4 of 4. Depends on [#13604](https://github.com/warpdotdev/warp/pull/13604).

Prevents multiline clipboard content from being interpreted as a sequence of Enter keypresses and immediately submitted by the TUI. The runtime now enables terminal bracketed-paste mode, preserves each crossterm paste payload as one structured `TuiEvent`, and inserts it into editable inputs atomically.

This matches GUI editor behavior: internal and trailing newlines remain intact, read-only editor surfaces ignore paste, and submission happens only after a separate Enter keypress.

### In scope

- Enable bracketed paste when entering TUI terminal mode and disable it during guaranteed terminal restoration.
- Convert `CrosstermEvent::Paste` into a dedicated payload-preserving `TuiEvent::Paste`.
- Route editable paste through one `InsertText(String)` action and one `CodeEditorModel::user_insert` call.
- Keep paste separate from keybindings, submission, and the typed-only `!` shell-mode trigger.
- Add focused conversion, lifecycle, editable/read-only editor, and no-submit regression coverage.

### Out of scope

- Clipboard ownership or `/export-to-clipboard` behavior; this fixes paste ingestion for all sources.
- Timing-based detection of unbracketed terminal input.
- Flattening or trimming pasted newlines.
- GUI editor changes.

### How to review

1. Start with `crates/warpui_core/src/runtime/mod.rs` for bracketed-paste lifecycle and restoration.
2. Review `crates/warpui_core/src/runtime/event_conversion.rs` and `crates/warpui_core/src/elements/tui/event.rs` for exact payload preservation.
3. Review `crates/warp_tui/src/editor_element.rs` for the editable-only event-to-action boundary.
4. Review `crates/warp_tui/src/input/view.rs` for the single atomic model insertion and absence of submission behavior.
5. Finish with the focused tests in the adjacent `*_tests.rs` files.

author: Moira Huang
message:
Align TUI slash command menu styling (#13604)

## Description

**Stack:** 3 of 4. Depends on [#13603](https://github.com/warpdotdev/warp/pull/13603); followed by [#13612](https://github.com/warpdotdev/warp/pull/13612).

Aligns the TUI slash-command menu with the intended visual treatment. Slash-command rows gain dedicated command, description, selection-background, and contrast-derived selected-text styles. Command and description columns are allocated once per menu from the available terminal width: the established 29-column alignment remains at ordinary widths, long titles expand in wide panes, and very narrow panes fall back to title-only rendering instead of starving descriptions.

Long command names use Unicode display-width-aware ASCII ellipses, including safe `.`/`..` degradation at extremely narrow widths. Recognized command and skill prefixes use the same command color in the input.

The input highlight is implemented as a reusable character-range overlay in `TuiEditorElement`, so it follows wrapped display rows without baking slash-command policy into the editor primitive. Parameterized static commands also render dim trailing argument hints without inserting them into the buffer; the GUI and TUI derive those hints from the same `StaticCommand::argument_hint()` metadata.

This PR does not change result ranking or ordering. The TUI continues to render the shared logical result order above the input, matching the GUI's above-input behavior. The Figma frame's top-row selection is treated as a static depicted state, not as an ordering requirement.

### In scope

- Add a slash-command row presentation mode with responsive, menu-wide command and description columns.
- Preserve ordinary-width alignment while expanding titles in wide panes and using a title-only narrow fallback.
- Add theme-derived styles for command names, selected-row backgrounds, and high-contrast selected text.
- Truncate long command names with Unicode display-width-aware ASCII ellipses while preserving useful description space.
- Add generic character-range text overrides to the char-cell editor, including soft-wrap-aware projection.
- Highlight only recognized static-command and skill prefixes in the input.
- Render static-command argument hints as dim trailing ghost text.
- Share static-command hint prefixes and text with the GUI registration path.
- Add render-to-lines and style coverage for ordinary, wide, boundary, narrow, Unicode, selection, theme, wrapped overrides, prefix highlighting, and ghost hints.

### Out of scope

- Result ranking, result reversal, or selection-index changes.
- Mouse interaction; keyboard-only interaction remains unchanged.
- Pixel-exact full-screen screenshot parity. Tests validate the design's semantic layout and color treatment in terminal cells.
- Semantic command IDs or richer GUI-style saved-prompt argument editing.

### How to review

1. Start with `crates/warp_tui/src/tui_builder.rs` for semantic command and selection styles, especially contrast derivation on custom themes.
2. Review `crates/warp_tui/src/inline_menu.rs` for shared responsive column allocation, narrow fallback, Unicode-aware truncation, and selected-row treatment. Rows continue to be consumed in logical order.
3. Review `crates/warp_tui/src/inline_menu_tests.rs` for preserved 50-column alignment plus 80-column, 20-column boundary, 19-column fallback, wide-character, and two-column ellipsis cases.
4. Review `crates/warp_tui/src/editor_element.rs` as generic editor capabilities: character ranges project through the display lattice, while trailing ghost text paints only at the end cursor.
5. Review `app/src/search/slash_command_menu/static_commands/mod.rs` and `app/src/terminal/input.rs` for shared argument-hint metadata and GUI usage.
6. Review `crates/warp_tui/src/slash_commands.rs` and `crates/warp_tui/src/input/view.rs` for the narrow domain-to-presentation bridge that computes recognized-prefix styling and hint visibility.

author: Matthew Albright
message:
Remove OrchestrationViewerStreamer and OwnerOrchestrationAncestorStreamer flags (#13659)

## Description

Removes two feature flags that have been in the default feature set
(enabled in all builds) since they stabilized.

## What

**OrchestrationViewerStreamer**: Removes the legacy REST polling path
from OrchestrationViewerModel (fetch_children, apply_children_fetch,
schedule_next_poll, maybe_kick_polling and the associated struct
fields/constants). The SSE-driven path via OrchestrationEventStreamer is
now unconditional. Also drops the flag guard in
stop_orchestration_polling in terminal_manager.rs.

**OwnerOrchestrationAncestorStreamer**: Parents now always use
AncestorRunId { include_self: true } in desired_sse_filter. The
UnsupportedRunIdCount variant, its report_error! handler, and
MAX_RUN_ID_STREAM_FILTER are removed — the RunIds parent fallback is
unreachable because a parent always has self_run_id by the time children
exist (the server must assign the parent a run before any child can be
created). The RunIds fallback is retained only for non-parent
(child-only) conversations.

## Why

Both flags were always on in production. Removing them eliminates ~750
lines of dead code and simplifies the SSE filter logic.

## Testing

- cargo clippy -p warp --tests -- -D warnings passes
- 94 tests across the three affected modules pass
(orchestration_viewer_model, orchestration_event_streamer,
terminal_manager)
- Legacy polling-path tests deleted; flag override guards removed from
remaining tests; two now-invalid without-flag streamer tests deleted

message:
[TUI] Keep transcript selection active while dragging past viewport edges (#13596)

## Description

Adds event-driven vertical scrolling while selecting text in the TUI
transcript. Each `LeftMouseDragged` event at the top or bottom edge
scrolls the viewport by one row, then updates the selection focus to the
newly visible content. The selection remains active until mouse-up, when
the existing copy callback materializes and copies the selected text.

The change also removes rendered-glyph snapshot invalidation. Streaming
or cosmetic glyph updates no longer clear an otherwise valid selection;
structural invalidation remains based on width changes and reported row
resizes.

This intentionally does not implement timer-driven or continuous
scrolling: holding the pointer stationary at an edge does not continue
scrolling.

Add low-effort slash commands to TUI (#13603)

## Description

**Stack:** 2 of 4. Depends on
[#13602](https://github.com/warpdotdev/warp/pull/13602); followed by
[#13604](https://github.com/warpdotdev/warp/pull/13604).

Adds slash-command effects that the TUI can support without GUI-only
panes or orchestration UI: `/create-new-project`,
`/export-to-clipboard`, `/export-to-file`, and `/cost`.

This keeps TUI-specific state transitions local while sharing non-visual
behavior with the GUI where practical. Project and review-comment
requests go through `BlocklistAIController`, conversation-file export
moves into a shared helper, and `/cost` validation is shared. Clipboard
export uses OSC 52 so text reaches the terminal client's clipboard
rather than the headless process host, including tmux passthrough and
explicit failure reporting. Successful clipboard and file exports show
transient feedback; file feedback includes the destination path and
whether an existing file was overwritten.

The PR also includes TUI request and execution plumbing for
`/pr-comments`, but that entry is not currently visible in the local
TUI. The GUI has migrated `/pr-comments` to a bundled skill, while local
`./script/run-tui` does not yet stage or load bundled skill resources.
Fixing local bundled-skill availability is separate follow-up work.

The `/compact` experience is completed with streamed summary rendering
and a dedicated progress label.

### In scope

- Expose and execute `/create-new-project`, `/export-to-clipboard`,
`/export-to-file`, and `/cost` in the TUI.
- Add the TUI request and execution plumbing needed by `/pr-comments`.
- Propagate repository and CWD context needed by repository-aware
commands and file export.
- Extract conversation Markdown export policy from GUI input code for
reuse by the TUI.
- Export clipboard text through OSC 52, including tmux passthrough and
clipboard/PRIMARY targets.
- Show success-colored export confirmation and report clipboard/file
failures accurately.
- Share project-creation, review-comment request, and cost-validation
behavior with GUI paths.
- Render streamed conversation summaries as collapsible transcript
sections.
- Label compaction progress distinctly.
- Add focused coverage for export behavior and feedback, command
support, cost validation, OSC 52 encoding, summary rendering, and
progress labels.

### Out of scope

- GUI-only commands that require settings panes, dialogs, or inline
selectors.
- Bundled-skill availability in the local TUI; `/pr-comments` remains
hidden until that infrastructure is available.
- Semantic command IDs or a cross-frontend handler abstraction.
- GUI-style structured argument editing for saved prompts.

### How to review

1. Start with `app/src/terminal/input/slash_commands/mod.rs` and
`app/src/ai/blocklist/controller.rs` for the supported-command boundary
and shared non-visual operations.
2. Review `app/src/ai/conversation_export.rs` alongside the removal from
`app/src/terminal/input.rs`. Confirm filename, CWD fallback, overwrite
reporting, and error behavior remain shared policy rather than TUI
policy.
3. Review `crates/warp_tui/src/terminal_session_view.rs` for
surface-specific command effects, validation, buffer clearing, export
feedback, and telemetry.
4. Review `crates/warp_tui/src/clipboard.rs` and `clipboard_tests.rs`
for OSC 52 encoding, target selection, flushing, and tmux passthrough.
5. Review `crates/warp_tui/src/agent_block.rs`,

6. Finish with the focused tests for export destinations, command

author: Yunfan Yang
message:
Fix TUI scroll position anchoring (#13651)

## Description

TUI viewports use `End` to follow content growth and `RowsFromTop` when
anchored to an absolute row. When collapsing content at the bottom made
a positive `RowsFromTop` offset exactly equal to the new maximum scroll
position, the viewport remained visually at the bottom but did not
transition back to `End`. Subsequent content growth therefore left the
viewport behind.

This change restores the end anchor when a positive absolute offset is
at or beyond the resolved maximum. It preserves `RowsFromTop(0)` as an
explicit top-aligned position when all content fits in the viewport.

A regression test reproduces the boundary case by collapsing a bottom
item so the requested offset equals the new maximum, then growing it
again to verify the viewport follows the new end.

author: Harry Albert
message:
conversation persistence and restoration for TUI (#13590)

## Description

Before this PR, exiting the Warp TUI discarded the only user-visible
path back to the selected conversation: the TUI accepted no
prior-conversation identifier, could not materialize historical
exchanges into its transcript, and lost the server token when the
full-screen app tore down. This PR adds an explicit resume loop around
the existing Oz conversation lifecycle. `warp-tui --resume
SERVER_CONVERSATION_TOKEN` validates the token before launch, carries it
through login, then restores the matching conversation from local
persistence or the server. The TUI now eagerly creates and activates its
session conversation at launch, so shell commands run before the first
prompt belong to that conversation and the first prompt cannot silently
create a different one. While resume is loading, the interactive empty
state is replaced by a centered loading view; malformed tokens fail
before launch, non-Oz conversations get an explicit unsupported-harness
error, and other load failures remain visible instead of silently
opening a new conversation. A successful restore reconstructs visible
prompts, agent output, recorded tool state, and conversation-derived
command blocks in canonical order, scrolls to the newest content,
focuses the input, and makes the next prompt continue the same server
conversation. On successful exit with a non-empty selected conversation,
the TUI prints the exact resume command after the host terminal has been
restored; an eager conversation for which the user never submitted a
prompt produces no hint, and no hint is printed when no usable selected
token exists.

The implementation is split at the layer that owns each part of
restoration. `session.rs` owns CLI parsing, carrying the token across
authentication, starting restoration only after the terminal surface is
wired, and printing the post-teardown resume hint through
`TuiExitSummaryHandle`. `TuiConversationSelection` owns the eager
selected conversation, keeps it aligned with `BlocklistAIHistoryModel`
and terminal command provenance, and creates eager replacements for
`/new` or removed conversations. `TuiTerminalSessionView` owns the
single-surface restore transition: it loads and validates the requested
Oz conversation before mutation, removes provisional
transcript/history/action state, prepares command and exchange
placement, restores historical action state, registers and activates the
conversation, materializes its TUI blocks, then selects it and focuses
input. The frontend-independent planner in
`app/src/terminal/conversation_restoration.rs` rebuilds serialized
command blocks, applies the GUI's hidden/task filtering, and computes
each exchange's placement relative to command timestamps; both GUI and
TUI consume that plan. `TranscriptScope` separates terminal transcript
visibility from GUI `AgentViewState`: the TUI uses `Unfiltered`, while
`AgentViewController` maps GUI inline/fullscreen transitions into
terminal or conversation scopes. Cloud context remains attached to the
generic active conversation context, so cloud-mode call sites no longer
need an `AgentViewState` argument. History clear events are
conversation-ID-scoped: delayed cleanup for the provisional conversation
can arrive after restoration, so selection and transcript subscribers
remove only IDs carried by the event instead of clearing the newly
restored conversation. No restored command is executed and no PTY or
shell process is recreated.

The most important files to look at are:
- `specs/CODE-1820/PRODUCT.md` and `specs/CODE-1820/TECH.md` — product
behavior, implemented flow, restoration ordering, and clear-event
invariants.
- `crates/warp_tui/src/session.rs` — `--resume` parsing, UUID
validation, login-gated startup restoration, and post-exit resume
output.
- `crates/warp_tui/src/conversation_selection.rs` — eager conversation
creation/activation, strict conversation targeting, `/new`, and ID-aware
history reconciliation.
- `crates/warp_tui/src/terminal_session_view.rs` — loading/failure
states, provisional conversation replacement, action/history
registration, selection, input focus, and continuation.
- `app/src/terminal/conversation_restoration.rs` — shared GUI/TUI
command reconstruction, exchange filtering, and timestamp-based block
placement.
- `crates/warp_tui/src/transcript_view.rs` — live and restored
`TuiAIBlock` materialization, placement, and conversation-ID-scoped
cleanup.
- `app/src/terminal/model/block.rs` and
`app/src/terminal/model/blocks.rs` — `TranscriptScope`, active
conversation provenance/cloud context, and canonical block-height
filtering.
- `crates/warp_tui/src/resume.rs` — selected non-empty conversation
token state that survives application teardown.
- `app/src/ai/blocklist/action_model.rs` — restoration and clearing of
recorded historical action results.
- `crates/warp_tui/src/ui.rs` — non-interactive loading and explicit
restoration-failure presentation.

Most other touched files do not need close review:
- `app/src/ai/blocklist/agent_view/agent_input_footer/mod.rs`,
`agent_message_bar.rs`, `block/status_bar.rs`,
`app/src/terminal/input.rs`, and the related `TerminalView` call sites
only adopt the simplified `is_in_cloud_context(&TerminalModel)` call
profile; they do not change cloud-mode behavior.
- `app/src/terminal/block_list_element.rs`, `block_list_viewport.rs`,
find/selection/shared-session callers, and their existing tests
mechanically replace `AgentViewState` visibility arguments with the
equivalent `TranscriptScope`; the visibility policy itself lives in
`app/src/terminal/model/block.rs` and `blocks.rs`.
- Integration and unit test files outside
`crates/warp_tui/src/conversation_selection_tests.rs`,
`transcript_view_tests.rs`, `resume_tests.rs`, and
`app/src/terminal/conversation_restoration_tests.rs` primarily update
fixtures and assertions for the new visibility type.
- `Cargo.lock`, `crates/warp_tui/Cargo.toml`, narrow export/visibility
changes in `app/src/tui_export.rs`, `history_model.rs`, and
`blocklist_filter.rs`, and the test-file move are dependency or plumbing
changes rather than restoration behavior.

## Testing

`./script/run-tui`

- [x] Focused command-restoration, delayed-clear, and empty-conversation
resume-hint regressions

warnings`

Remove leftover PRCommentsSlashCommand references breaking CI (#13650)

## Description
`FeatureFlag::PRCommentsSlashCommand` was removed in #13621 (*Remove the
/pr-comments slash command, superseded by the bundled skill*), but three
`let _flag_override =
FeatureFlag::PRCommentsSlashCommand.override_enabled(true);` statements
were left behind in `app/src/code_review/code_review_view_tests.rs`.

They reference a variant that no longer exists, so `cargo clippy
--workspace --exclude warp_completer --all-targets --tests` fails on
`master` with:

```
error[E0599]: no variant or associated item named `PRCommentsSlashCommand` found for enum `warp_core::features::FeatureFlag`
  --> app/src/code_review/code_review_view_tests.rs:828 / 869 / 911
error: could not compile `warp` (lib test) due to 3 previous errors
```

Because PR CI builds the merge of each PR with `master`, this breaks the
`Formatting + Clippy` and test jobs on **every** open PR, not just one.
This removes the three dead overrides (unused `_flag_override` bindings
whose flag is gone); the surrounding tests are unchanged.

- [x] Fixes a `master` CI breakage (no separate issue).

## Testing

before).

behavior change.

author: Moira Huang
message:
Share slash command behavior between GUI and TUI (#13602)

## Description

**Stack:** 1 of 4. Follow-up:
[#13603](https://github.com/warpdotdev/warp/pull/13603).

Establishes behavioral parity between the GUI and TUI for exact-match
slash-command menu visibility, acceptance telemetry, and submitted skill
invocation.

The TUI now parses submitted input before clearing it, so recognized
static commands and skills take their typed execution paths while
ordinary text remains an Agent prompt. Skill resolution and request
construction are extracted into `BlocklistAIController` and reused by
both GUI and TUI call sites.

Ordinary nonblank prompts are submitted without trimming their leading
whitespace. This preserves GUI parity and prevents input such as `
/compact` from regaining slash-command semantics after the shared parser
correctly rejects it.

### In scope

- Share the GUI's exact-match menu-closing rule with the TUI.
- Route static-command and saved-prompt acceptance telemetry through
shared helpers.
- Extract skill resolution and request construction from the GUI input
path.
- Use that shared controller path for TUI skill submissions.
- Classify submitted TUI input before clearing the editor.
- Preserve raw nonblank prompt text while rejecting whitespace-only
submissions.
- Add regression coverage for exact static-command and skill matches,
menu lifecycle, submitted-input routing, and leading-whitespace
preservation.

### Out of scope

- Semantic command IDs.
- GUI-style structured editing for parameterized saved prompts.

### How to review

1. Start with `app/src/terminal/input/slash_commands/mod.rs` for the
newly extracted exact-match and telemetry helpers.
2. Review `app/src/ai/blocklist/controller.rs` together with
`app/src/terminal/input.rs`; this should be a behavior-preserving
extraction of the existing GUI skill path.
3. Review `crates/warp_tui/src/slash_commands.rs` for the TUI
integration of the shared exact-match rule.
4. Review `crates/warp_tui/src/terminal_session_view.rs` for
submitted-input classification, raw-prompt preservation, and TUI skill
invocation.
5. Finish with `app/src/tui_export.rs`,
`app/src/terminal/input/slash_command_model_tests.rs`, and
`crates/warp_tui/src/terminal_session_view_tests.rs` for the exported
boundary and parity coverage.

No linked issue.

Remove the /pr-comments slash command (superseded by the bundled skill) (#13621)

## Description
Removes the legacy `/pr-comments` slash command now that the bundled
`pr-comments` skill ships the same PR-review-comment flow.

The command was only ever registered when `PRCommentsSlashCommand` was
enabled **and** `PRCommentsSkill` was disabled, and `pr_comments_skill`
is in the app's default feature set — so default builds already used the
bundled skill exclusively. This PR deletes the slash command and its
dedicated backend and removes the `PRCommentsSlashCommand` feature flag.

What's removed:
- The `/pr-comments` `StaticCommand`, its registration, and its input
handler.
- The `FetchReviewComments` request pipeline — the slash-command handler
was its only producer — across `SlashCommandRequest`, `AIAgentInput`,
the API convert/persistence/telemetry/redaction layers, and the warping
indicator.
- The `PRCommentsSlashCommand` feature flag (`warp_features`,
`features.rs`, `Cargo.toml`).

What's intentionally kept (shared with the bundled skill):
- The `insert_code_review_comments` tool
(`AIAgentActionType::InsertCodeReviewComments`) and its
`InsertReviewComments` tool declaration.
- The `PRCommentsV2` inline rendering of imported comments and the
"outdated comment" code-review behavior. These were gated behind
`PRCommentsSlashCommand` (default-on) and are now simply un-gated
(collapsed to the default-on branch) so the skill keeps working.

for back-compat: restoring an old conversation that used `/pr-comments`
no longer renders that historical input, but does not crash.

message:
Fix code review pinned file header leaking diff through its rounded corners (#13616)

## Description
Fixes the Code Review file header leaking diff content through its
rounded top corners while scrolling.

When you scroll within an expanded file in Code Review, the file header
pins to the top of the panel as a sticky overlay on top of the diff. The
header's opaque backing (`outer_bg`, the panel background) was drawn
with rounded top corners, so the corner notches were left transparent —
and the green/red diff scrolling underneath showed through the header's
rounded corners.

The inner header keeps its rounded corners for the card look. This
change squares off **only the pinned header's** outer backing so its
opaque panel background fills the corner notches (matching what the
corners reveal at rest). It is scoped to the pinned header via a new
`is_pinned` argument to `render_file_header`, so the at-rest card is
visually unchanged.

Critically, this does **not** clip or re-margin the diff content. The
earlier fix for this exact bug (#13091) wrapped the diff in a `Clipped`
region with a negative top margin; because `Container::layout` subtracts
margins from the child's size constraint, that negative margin inflated
the editor's minimum height and — via the editor's
`constrain_absolute_children` overlays — made the selection "Add as
context" popup and the inline comment box render oversized while
scrolled. That is why #13091 was reverted (#13194). This approach avoids
touching layout constraints entirely, so it can't reintroduce that
regression.

## Linked Issue
N/A — reported via the factory-client Slack triage channel.
- [ ] The linked issue is labeled `ready-to-spec` or
`ready-to-implement`.
- [x] Where appropriate, screenshots or a short video of the
implementation are included below (especially for user-visible or UI
changes).

## Testing
- `./script/format` passes.
- `cargo check -p warp --features local_fs` passes.
- `cargo clippy -p warp --all-targets --features local_fs --tests -- -D
warnings` passes.
- **Manual UI verification with computer use (recording):** built and
launched Warp, opened Code Review on a repo with a tall all-green
new-file diff, and scrolled within the file so its header pins.
Confirmed the pinned header's rounded top corners show clean panel
background with no green content bleeding through the corners, during
active scrolling and at rest. The card's bottom corners and overall
layout look normal, and no oversized selection-popup / comment-box
appeared (i.e. the #13091 regression does not recur). A screen recording
of the verification is attached in the Slack thread.
- No automated test added: this is a paint-level corner-rendering
behavior on the sticky header overlay that isn't practically covered by

author: Max Hsu
message: Add Reminders and full-access EventKit usage descriptions to Info.plist (#13608) ## Description `icalBuddy` (and other EventKit-based CLIs) fail with `error: No calendars.` inside Warp while working in Terminal.app. As @jagmit diagnosed in #13558, the cause is the generated bundle `Info.plist`: macOS attributes a CLI's permission requests to the terminal app (the TCC "responsible process") and only shows a consent prompt if that app declares the matching usage-description key. `icalBuddy` opens both the event store and the **reminders** store at startup, but Warp's plist declared only `NSCalendarsUsageDescription` — no Reminders key, and none of the macOS 14 full-access variants. The reminders request was therefore denied silently: no prompt, and no System Settings entry the user could flip. This adds the missing keys to `script/update_plist`: - `NSCalendarsFullAccessUsageDescription` - `NSRemindersUsageDescription` - `NSRemindersFullAccessUsageDescription` matching the existing description style. No other behavior changes; not a UI/rendering change. ## Linked Issue Closes #13558 ## Testing Ran the permission-insert block from `script/update_plist` against a fresh plist and confirmed the three new keys are inserted with the correct strings and the result is a valid plist: ``` $ plutil -extract NSRemindersUsageDescription raw Info.plist A program in Warp wants to use your reminders. $ plutil -extract NSRemindersFullAccessUsageDescription raw Info.plist A program in Warp wants full access to your reminders. $ plutil -extract NSCalendarsFullAccessUsageDescription raw Info.plist A program in Warp wants full access to your calendar. $ plutil -lint Info.plist Info.plist: OK ``` `bash -n script/update_plist` passes. ## Visual evidence — consent prompt + Settings entry macOS shows the reminders consent prompt **only if the responsible app's `Info.plist` declares the matching key** — otherwise the request is silently denied, which is this bug. The check below issues the same reminders request (`EKEventStore.requestFullAccessToReminders`, the same API path `icalBuddy` takes) under two Warp builds that differ **only** in the plist. **Before** — build without the keys (current behavior): silently denied, no prompt, no Settings entry. ``` Reminders authorization status (before request): notDetermined Requesting Reminders access via EventKit ... -> granted=false status=notDetermined error=nil RESULT: no reminders access (silently denied — the reported bug) ``` **After** — build with this PR's keys: the consent prompt appears, and its body is verbatim the `NSRemindersFullAccessUsageDescription` string added in this PR. Granting it, Reminders access succeeds and the app appears under **System Settings → Privacy & Security → Reminders**: ``` Requesting Reminders access via EventKit ... -> granted=true status=authorized error=nil RESULT: reminders accessible ✅ ``` The screens are from a WarpOss dev bundle with this PR's `script/update_plist` delta applied to its `Info.plist` and re-signed — the binary is untouched, since this change is plist-only. A signed release build produces the same prompt from the same keys; an external contributor can't sign a release, so this demonstrates the mechanism on a local ad-hoc-signed build.

message:
Set HOMEBREW_NO_ASK in macOS bootstrap for non-interactive brew installs (#13638)

## Description
Homebrew made ask-by-default behavior the standard, so an interactive
`brew install` now prints a plan and prompts for confirmation before
downloading/installing. That prompt is TTY-gated (Homebrew skips it
without a TTY), so it blocks a developer running
`script/macos/bootstrap` in their terminal — the script would hang
waiting for input.

This sets `export HOMEBREW_NO_ASK=1` once near the top of
`script/macos/bootstrap` so every `brew install` in the script
(including the `--cask` and `google-cloud-sdk` installs, and any added
later) runs non-interactively. `HOMEBREW_NO_ASK` is Homebrew's
documented opt-out for ask-by-default.

**Audit of other scripts (why only this one changed):**
- CI `brew install` steps (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`,
`.github/workflows/create_release.yml`,
`.github/actions/prepare_environment/action.yml`) run without a TTY, so
Homebrew auto-skips the prompt — no hang, no change needed.
- Other `brew install` occurrences in `script/bootstrap`,
`script/wasm/bundle`, `script/deploy_remote_server*` are comments or
echoed instructions, not invocations the script runs.
- `brew update` has no `--ask` prompt.

## Linked Issue

No GitHub issue.

## Testing

author: warp-dev-github-integration[bot]
message:
Fix stray characters in TUI transcript from wide-grapheme cursor shift (#13591)

## Description
Fixes stray/stale characters that appear in the TUI transcript and
persist while scrolling a long transcript (reported with floating `g`
glyphs that were not part of the agent output, e.g. rendered mid-word as
`inline` `g` `task`).

**Root cause.** The transcript is not fully repainted every frame —
`TuiFrameRenderer::draw` (`crates/warpui_core/src/runtime/renderer.rs`)
only clears + repaints on the first frame or on a resize; scrolling
within a fixed viewport is an incremental cell diff. The proximate
defect is in the `ratatui-crossterm` backend: `CrosstermBackend::draw`
suppresses the cursor `MoveTo` for any cell whose column is exactly one
past the previously written cell, assuming every printed cell advances
the cursor by a single column. A width-2 grapheme advances it by two.
`Buffer::diff` normally skips a wide grapheme's trailing half, which
masks the bug — **except** for emoji carrying a VS16 variation selector
(U+FE0F, e.g. ⚠️ ⌨️ ✅ ▶️), where the diff deliberately emits a
trailing-clear cell at `wide_x + 1`. That cell lands in the
false-contiguity window, so the backend skips the needed `MoveTo` and
prints it — and everything after it on the line — one column too far
right. Because scrolling only redraws the per-frame diff, the misplaced
glyph is never repaired and sticks around. This is why the bug is flaky
and content-dependent: it only triggers when the transcript contains
VS16 emoji, which are common in agent output.

**Fix.** Emit the per-cell diff in batches that never span a wide
grapheme: after a width-2 grapheme, a fresh `backend.draw()` call resets
the backend's cursor tracking so the following cell gets a correct
`MoveTo`. The change is contained to the renderer, preserves the
synchronized-update wrapper and ratatui's style handling, and leaves the
width-1 contiguous-run optimization intact.

## Linked Issue
Reported via the factory-client bug-triage Slack thread (linked below).
No GitHub issue.

## Testing
- Added regression test

message:
Fix imported PR comments on context lines wrongly marked outdated (#13585)

## Description
Imported GitHub PR review comments were being shown as **Outdated** (and
omitted from what's sent to the agent) in Warp's Code Review pane when
the comment was placed on a **context (unchanged)** diff line — even
though the line still exists and GitHub doesn't consider it outdated.
Comments on added/removed lines were unaffected, which made the behavior
look inconsistent.

**Root cause:** Warp computes "outdated" locally by re-locating each
imported comment's line in the current diff via an exact string match.
Imported comments store the raw unified-diff line *including* its
one-char prefix marker (`+`, `-`, or a leading space for context lines).
`LineDiffContent::original_text()` strips a leading `+` or `-` but not
the context space, so relocation compared e.g. `" active_repo_root:
Option<PathBuf>,"` against the file's `"active_repo_root:
Option<PathBuf>,"` → never matched → `get_new_line_location` returned
`used_fallback = true` → the comment was marked outdated (gated by
`FeatureFlag::PRCommentsSlashCommand`).

**Fix (minimal, origin-gated):** `original_text()` is shared with
*native* comments, whose context content stores genuine leading
indentation, so it can't simply strip the space. This PR adds
`LineDiffContent::imported_original_text()`, which strips exactly one
leading `+`/`-`/space marker, and uses it for `ImportedFromGitHub`
comments in `relocate_comments`. Native comments keep `original_text()`,
so indented native lines still match. No stored `content` is changed
(what's sent to the agent/GitHub is untouched) — only the transient text
used for line matching.

## Linked Issue
Reported via the factory-client Slack thread (linked below). No GitHub
issue.
- [ ] The linked issue is labeled `ready-to-spec` or
`ready-to-implement`.
- [x] Where appropriate, screenshots or a short video of the
implementation are included below.

## Testing
Added tests that fail before and pass after the fix:
- `test_imported_context_line_comment_relocates_and_not_outdated` —
imported context-line comment on a line that still exists → not
outdated, no fallback.
- `test_imported_context_line_comment_removed_marked_outdated` —
imported context-line comment whose line is gone → still correctly
outdated (fallback preserved).
- `test_native_indented_context_comment_not_outdated` — native
indented-line comment still matches (no regression from the fix).
- `imported_original_text_*` unit tests in `comment_tests.rs` covering
`" line 2"`→`line 2`, `" indented"`→`" indented"`, `"+add"`→`add`,
`"-del"`→`del`, `" "`→`""`, `"+- list"`→`- list`.

Validation run (single build job): all targeted tests pass (203
including the existing `original_text_*`, `diff_hunk_parser`, and
`relocate_comments` tests); `cargo fmt` clean; `cargo clippy -p warp
--all-targets --tests -- -D warnings` clean.

*Outdated*.

message:
Fix repo-gated slash commands lingering after leaving a repository (#13614)

## Description
Repo-gated slash commands (`/pr-comments`, `/open-code-review`,
`/index`, `/init`, `/open-project-rules`) are gated on
`Availability::REPOSITORY`, which was derived from a **cached**
`active_repo_root` in `SlashCommandDataSource::base_availability`. That
cache is only refreshed after the **async** `detect_possible_git_repo`
spawn resolves, but the pwd-changed recompute
(`ActiveSessionEvent::UpdatedPwd`) runs immediately on `cd`. As a
result, repo-gated commands remained available in the stale window after
moving from a repository into a non-repository directory, until
detection completed.

This change derives `REPOSITORY` from the **live** working directory via
`DetectedRepositories::get_root_for_path` (reusing the app's centralized
path canonicalization + ancestor walk), gated on local sessions. This
closes the window without waiting on async detection, and reuses
existing cross-platform path handling rather than a hand-rolled ancestor
check. `active_repo_root`/`set_active_repo_root` are retained purely as
the recompute trigger for the non-repo→repo case (a freshly-entered repo
isn't in the cache until detection completes and fires a recompute).

Chosen over an eager-clear-on-`cd` approach, which would flicker the
command off/on when moving *within* the same repo (guarded by a control
test here).

## Linked Issue
Reported via factory-client Slack triage (no GitHub issue). Slack
thread:
https://warpdev.slack.com/archives/C0BCE7AELJ2/p1783792807637709?thread_ts=1783792807.637709&cid=C0BCE7AELJ2
- [ ] The linked issue is labeled `ready-to-spec` or
`ready-to-implement`.

implementation are included below.

`repository_gated_command_drops_when_leaving_repository` — fails before
the fix, passes after. Asserts a `REPOSITORY`-gated command is inactive
immediately after `UpdatedPwd` to a non-repo dir while the stale cache
is still set.
- [x] **No-flicker control:**
`repository_gated_command_stays_within_repository` — passes; `cd` within
the same repo keeps the command active.
- [x] Both tests pass: `CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 cargo
nextest run -p warp --lib repository_gated_command_` → `2 passed`.
- [x] `cargo fmt` and `cargo clippy -p warp --lib --tests -- -D

message:
Build bundled skills when running ./script/run-tui (#13618)

## Description
`./script/run-tui` built the TUI without the `standalone` feature and
never staged bundled resources next to the binary. At runtime the app
resolves bundled skills via `warp_core::paths::bundled_resources_dir()`,
which points at a `resources/` directory **next to the executable** (and
on macOS only does so when built with the `standalone` feature). Because
a dev `run-tui` binary lives at `target/<profile>/warp-tui[-oss]` with
no sibling `resources/`, bundled skills could not be resolved when
running locally.

The packaged TUI (`script/macos/bundle`, `ARTIFACT=tui`) already handles
this: it builds with `release_bundle,standalone` and runs
`prepare_bundled_resources` next to the binary. This PR mirrors that in
the dev run script:

- Build with `--features standalone` so the sibling `resources/` dir is
the resolution target on all platforms.
- Stage bundled skills into `target/<profile>/resources` via
`prepare_bundled_resources` before launch, skipping license generation
and the settings schema (`NO_LICENSES=1 SKIP_SETTINGS_SCHEMA=1`) to keep
dev runs fast — matching `script/macos/run`.
- The Linux/Windows path now builds then execs the binary directly
(instead of `cargo run`) so resources can be staged next to the built
binary before launch.

## Linked Issue
Reported in the factory-client bug-triage Slack thread (linked below).

## Testing
This change touches only the `script/run-tui` shell script (no Rust
code), so the Rust-specific mandates (`./script/presubmit`, regression
tests) don't apply. Validation performed:

- `bash -n script/run-tui` — passes.
- Ran `NO_LICENSES=1 SKIP_SETTINGS_SCHEMA=1
./script/prepare_bundled_resources <tmp>/resources oss` (the exact
invocation the script uses) and confirmed the bundled skills are staged
under `<tmp>/resources/bundled/skills` (add-mcp-server,
change-keybinding, claude-api, create-skill, create-tab-config,
modify-settings, oz-platform, pr-comments, tab-configs,
update-tab-config, warpctrl).

A full `standalone` TUI compile was not run here (heavy/OOM-prone in
this environment); the `standalone` feature and the resource-staging
step are the same ones the production TUI bundle already uses.

author: warp-dev-github-integration[bot]
message:
Fix wasm debug panic in StandardizedPath::from_local_absolute_unchecked (#13552)

## Description
Fixes a debug-only panic in the **wasm build** reported in the
factory-client bug-triage channel:

```
panicked at crates/warp_util/src/standardized_path.rs:78:9:
from_local_absolute called with non-absolute path: /Users/david/src/warp/crates/computer_use/src/mac/mod.rs
```

### Root cause
`StandardizedPath::from_local_absolute_unchecked` guarded its input with
`std::path::Path::is_absolute()`. Warp's wasm build targets
`wasm32-unknown-unknown`, and on that target std treats a Unix-rooted
path like `/Users/...` as **not** absolute: the target is neither
`cfg(unix)` nor `wasi`, so std only considers a path absolute when it
has a Windows-style prefix (e.g. `C:`). As a result a genuinely-absolute
path tripped the `debug_assert!` and panicked in debug wasm builds.
(Release builds were unaffected — it's a `debug_assert!`.)

The other constructors in this module (`try_new`, `try_from_local`, …)
don't hit this because they check absoluteness via the `typed_path`
crate, which is encoding-aware and correct on every target.

### Fix
Make the debug guard use the same encoding-aware `typed_path`
absoluteness check the rest of the module already uses, instead of std's
`is_absolute()`. The `to_str()` (UTF-8) debug assert is preserved.

## Testing
- Added regression tests
`from_local_absolute_unchecked_accepts_absolute` and
`from_local_absolute_unchecked_normalizes` in
`standardized_path_tests.rs`.
- `cargo test -p warp_util standardized_path` — all 23 tests pass.
- `cargo clippy -p warp_util --all-targets --all-features --tests -- -D
warnings` — clean.
- `./script/format` — clean.

Note: the true fail-before repro is wasm-target-specific and can't be
reproduced by running native tests on the CI/dev host (on native Unix
`Path::is_absolute()` already returns true). The root cause is
deterministic from std's path implementation on
`wasm32-unknown-unknown`, and the fix removes the std `is_absolute()`
dependency entirely.

author: warp-dev-github-integration[bot]
message:
Cache TUI transcript block heights to fix long-transcript scroll lag (#13592)

## Description
Fixes scrolling/viewport lag in the warp-tui **transcript view** with
long transcripts containing many AI blocks (collapsible thinking items,
agent shell commands, etc.).

**Root cause (viewporting inefficiency, not scroll handling).** On every
frame (each scroll tick and idle repaint),
`TuiBlockListViewportSource::visible_items` re-measured *every* agent
block in a viewport±20-row overhang band by fully rebuilding and laying
out each block's element tree via `TuiAIBlock::desired_height` — with no
height cache keyed by width. The block list already stored
`last_laid_out_height`, but the viewport threw that cache away for the
measurement decision. As transcripts grow (bigger/more thinking + shell
blocks), the fixed per-frame band cost balloons and scrolling feels
laggy. This mirrors the GUI blocklist prior art, except the GUI
re-lays-out *retained* elements (cheap when unchanged) while the TUI
rebuilt immediate-mode trees from scratch every frame.

**Fix.** Gate the overhang re-measure on staleness. A non-dirty band
block is re-measured only when its cached height cannot be trusted:
- its layout **width changed** since it was last measured (reflow), or
it was never measured at a known width; or
- it is **still streaming** (its height can grow without a per-update
invalidation — e.g. an expanded, still-running shell command).

At a stable width with nothing streaming, the cached
`last_laid_out_height` is reused, so height write-back short-circuits
and the extra `model.lock()` acquisitions are skipped. Off-band blocks
keep their cached height until they scroll into the band, matching the
existing GUI overhang behavior.

To persist the width across frames (the viewport source is reconstructed
every render), `RichContentItem` gains a `measured_width`, recorded by
the row-based (TUI) height-update path; the GUI pixel path leaves it
`None` and is unaffected.

## Linked Issue
- [ ] The linked issue is labeled `ready-to-spec` or
`ready-to-implement`.
- [ ] Where appropriate, screenshots or a short video of the
implementation are included below (especially for user-visible or UI
changes).

https://warpdev.slack.com/archives/C0BCE7AELJ2/p1783722795932879?thread_ts=1783722795.932879&cid=C0BCE7AELJ2_

## Testing
Validation (all with `CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1`):
- [x] `cargo nextest run -p warp_tui` — 132 passed (incl. new tests
below)
- [x] `cargo nextest run -p warpui_core` — 310 passed

passed

--tests -- -D warnings` — clean

New / updated tests (in
`crates/warp_tui/src/tui_block_list_viewport_source_tests.rs`):
- `tui_transcript_scroll_reuses_cached_heights_at_stable_width` (new
regression guard): a non-dirty, non-streaming block seeded with a wrong
height at a stable width is **not** re-measured (no resize emitted), but
**is** re-measured on a width change and when marked dirty. This fails
before the fix (the old code re-measured every frame and corrected the
seeded height) and passes after.
- `tui_agent_streaming_block_remeasured_at_stable_width` (new): a
streaming block is still re-measured at a stable width, so live height
growth (e.g. an expanded running shell command) stays reflected.
- `tui_agent_overhang_remeasures_visible_non_dirty_height` updated to
the new contract: re-measures on first measure (no recorded width), and
a subsequent same-width frame does not re-measure.

Performance evidence: a headless benchmark over 30 agent blocks showed
steady-state frames drop from ~1790µs/frame to the ~56µs/frame
cached-reuse baseline (~32× less redundant work per frame in a debug
build).

UI verification note: the authenticated warp-tui transcript view cannot
be driven non-interactively in the cloud runner (interactive device-auth

regression test plus the headless benchmark are the gates; a human/CI

message:
Add Copy action to locked initial cloud-mode queued prompt (#13595)

## Description
While a cloud agent environment is being created, the **initial
cloud-mode prompt** in the queued-prompts panel is *locked* — it can't
be edited or deleted (tooltip: "The first cloud-mode prompt cannot be
changed."). But the row still showed **Edit** and **Delete** actions,
and deleting the locked row discarded the user's original prompt with no
way to recover it if environment setup fails.

For this specific locked row only, this PR **replaces the Delete action
with a Copy action** that writes the **full, original prompt**
(including long or multiline content — read straight from the model, not
the truncated single-line preview) to the clipboard. Send-now and Edit
remain in place (disabled, with their existing tooltips).

The change is narrowly scoped:
- Only the locked initial cloud-mode prompt row is affected.
- Local queued prompts and normal editable queued prompts are unchanged
and keep their existing Delete action and behavior.
- The trailing action count is unchanged (Send / Edit / Copy vs. Send /
Edit / Delete), so the row's reserved action footprint and layout are
unaffected.

Reported in the factory-client Slack thread.

## Linked Issue
N/A — reported via the factory-client bug-triage Slack channel.

## Testing
- Added a unit test
(`locked_initial_cloud_mode_prompt_shows_copy_action_that_copies_full_prompt`)
that asserts the locked initial cloud-mode row exposes a Copy action
(and ordinary rows do not), and that clicking Copy writes the **full**
prompt — verified with a long, multiline prompt — to the clipboard
verbatim, leaving the row in the queue.
- `./script/format` clean; `cargo clippy -p warp --lib` and `cargo
clippy -p warp --tests` both pass with `-D warnings`.
- **Manual UI verification** with the built dogfood app (`cargo run
--bin warp -- --api-key …`): reached the cloud-mode "Creating

On hover the row shows exactly three actions — up-arrow (Send now,

message:
Fix agent shell commands appearing twice in the TUI transcript (#13594)

## Description
In the TUI transcript, an agent-run shell command could appear twice:
once inline inside its agent block and again as a standalone terminal
block. Collapsing the inline command could also leave a large blank gap
after the command had previously been expanded.

Agent-requested command blocks are rendered inline by
`TuiShellCommandView`, but the same canonical terminal block also
participates in the top-level block list. When a long-running command
transitions to agent-monitored mode, that canonical block becomes
visible again, producing duplicate output and retaining top-level layout
height even when it is not painted.

This PR fixes both TUI-specific layout paths without changing GUI
behavior:

- When a CLI subagent spawns for an agent-requested command, the TUI
marks the canonical command block hidden in the block list. This
recomputes its top-level sum-tree height to zero while preserving the
same terminal content for the inline shell-command view.
- User-started monitored commands remain top-level-visible because they
do not have an initial requested-command action ID.
- Shell-command disclosure clicks now dispatch through a registered
typed-action child view.
- Expand/collapse changes emit a layout invalidation from
`TuiShellCommandView` to `TuiAIBlock`; the transcript then marks the
rich-content item dirty so its height and all following row origins are
remeasured.

## Linked Issue
Reported in the factory-client triage thread:
https://warpdev.slack.com/archives/C0BCE7AELJ2/p1783726341426129?thread_ts=1783726341.426129&cid=C0BCE7AELJ2

## Testing


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/28b95c75-1373-478b-93d5-78a12faa411d

Added regression coverage for:

- Agent-requested commands having zero top-level height after a CLI
subagent starts.
- User-started monitored commands retaining their top-level height.
- Hidden canonical command blocks leaving no viewport gap.
- Inline shell commands expanding and re-collapsing to their original
height through the typed-action path.
- Shell-command layout invalidation propagating to the owning agent
block.

`./script/run-tui` successfully builds and codesigns the updated TUI
binary for local testing.
